channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average
item.description: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Lines in mid-March at a gas station in Hawthorne, Calif.

item.title: La cúpula de Irán tiene dificultades para coordinarse, dicen funcionarios
item.description: Decenas de dirigentes iraníes han muerto desde que comenzó la guerra hace cuatro semanas, y aquellos que sobreviven han tenido dificultades para comunicarse.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Adam Goldman and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Muchos altos dirigentes iraníes y sus adjuntos han sido asesinados, y quienes sobreviven tienen dificultades para comunicarse.

item.title: Andy Weir habla sobre la ciencia detrás de ‘Proyecto Hail Mary’
item.description: El autor de ciencia ficción habla de ‘Proyecto Hail Mary”, su novela científica que fue adaptada en una película protagonizada por Ryan Gosling.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

media:content.media:credit: Jonathan Olley/Amazon MGM Studios, vía Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Andy Weir, autor del libro de ciencia ficción Proyecto Hail Mary, en el plató de la adaptación cinematográfica, que él mismo ha producido y que se estrena el viernes.

item.title: Picture Prompt | Favorite Podcasts
item.description: What do you like to listen to? Why?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Irene Rinaldi

item.title: The Push to Ban Cars From All New York City Parks
item.description: Central Park is car-free, and so is Prospect Park. What about the rest?
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu

media:content.media:credit: James Estrin/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Andy Smith, who lives near Freedom Drive in Forest Park, started a petition to ban cars there.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 31, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Astronauts Prepare for Artemis II Moon Mission
item.description: NASA is sending four astronauts to space on a mission to circle the moon and test a life support system. The astronauts spoke to The New York Times in January before the mission was delayed until April because of technical issues.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Bella, McKinnon de Kuyper and Daniel Fetherston

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Attack Hits Kuwaiti Tanker Full of Oil Near Dubai
item.description: The massive vessel caught fire after what its owner called an Iranian attack. On Monday, President Trump warned Iran that without a deal, he would order attacks on infrastructure targets.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Qué significa ‘equitable’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 212 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: equitable
item.description: This word has appeared in 212 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Stephen Colbert Marvels at ‘No Kings’ Rallies Worldwide
item.description: “Just look at the crowd we got here in New York,” he said. “Oop, that’s the T.S.A. line at J.F.K.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: Trump, Don’t Make Churchill’s Deadly Mistake
item.description: A 1936 convention establishing Turkish control over the Dardanelles could be an inspiration to resolving the energy bottleneck in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Asli Aydintasbas
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: El debate de la ciudadanía por derecho de nacimiento llega a la Corte Suprema
item.description: El miércoles, la Corte Suprema escuchará los argumentos sobre la legalidad de la orden ejecutiva de Trump, y algunos expertos legales conservadores afirman que podría ser un caso más disputado de lo que se pensaba.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow

item.title: When Will Japan’s Cherry Blossoms Bloom? A.I. Can Help Answer That
item.description: Experts use artificial intelligence to analyze data, plus thousands of crowdsourced photos, to forecast the prized flowers, which are a multibillion-dollar attraction.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Kiuko Notoya, Hisako Ueno and Kentaro Takahashi

media:content.media:description: Visitors gathered beneath cherry blossoms at Ueno Park in Tokyo last week.

item.title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
item.description: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman, Eric Schmitt and Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jaimi Joy/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at a meeting in London on Monday. “We will not be drawn into the wider war,” he told lawmakers in mid-March.

item.title: ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ on Broadway, With Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, Is Underbaked
item.description: A raucous adaptation of a gritty portrait of New York stifles tension with comedy, leaving its stars, Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, adrift.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jon Bernthal, center, as Sonny, with Jessica Hecht as the mouthy bank teller Colleen and Danny Johnson as a security guard in “Dog Day Afternoon” at the August Wilson Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Video Game Fans Issue a Plea To Filmmakers: Don’t Be Noobs
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, March 31, 2026.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 31, 2026
item.description: Pack your bags — Ryan Patrick Smith’s puzzle will take you for a spin.
item.dc:creator: Priya Krishna

media:content.media:credit: Stan Honda/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Looking for hints? We got you.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Sends Cease-and-Desist Letter to F.B.I. Over Case Files
item.description: A Trump administration effort to gather documents from an old investigation had alarmed law enforcement officials, who feared it would be used to smear the Democratic lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An investigation of Representative Eric Swalwell of California over a decade ago into a Chinese fund-raiser suspected of spying brought no charges.

item.title: Senators Seek Answers From Trump Administration About Airport Immigration Arrests
item.description: Airport security officials have been sharing passenger data with immigration agents, but the program received little attention until videos captured a woman and her daughter being detained at San Francisco’s airport.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Transportation Security Administration began sharing passenger information with immigration officials a year ago, highlighting fliers who were on a list of people to be deported.

item.title: Kuwaiti Oil Tanker Set Ablaze After Attack Off the Coast of Dubai
item.description: The attack damaged the hull, the owner of the tanker said, which could cause an oil spill.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Alfio Finocchiaro/Alamy
media:content.media:description: The Dubai port in the United Arab Emirates in 2022.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: Iran maintained that no negotiations have been held with the United States, and said none would while the fighting continues.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Residents viewing the damage to their apartment in Tehran after airstrikes on Monday.

item.title: She Was a Famous Millennial Feminist. Her Polyamory Memoir Is Heartbreaking.
item.description: Almost every ideology can be wielded to make women feel that they’re failing.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

item.title: After Months of Threats, Trump Softens His Stance on Blocking Oil to Cuba
item.description: The White House press secretary said the United States would evaluate oil shipments to Cuba on a “case-by-case basis,” after a Russian tanker full of crude reached the island.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Anton Troianovski and Maria Abi-Habib
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An oil tanker near the coastline of Matanzas, Cuba, on Monday. The ban on foreign oil imports has plunged the country into a crisis.

item.title: DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Florida Airport for Trump
item.description: The airport near West Palm Beach will be rebranded the President Donald J. Trump International Airport on July 1, if approved by the Federal Aviation Administration.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at Palm Beach International Airport in West Palm Beach, Fla., last month.

item.title: Why Barbie Dream Fest in Florida Is Drawing Comparisons to Fyre Festival
item.description: Fans had high hopes for the event, a Mattel-sanctioned gathering in a Florida convention center. Now, it’s drawing comparisons to Fyre Festival.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:credit: Tara Brooks
media:content.media:description: The bright pink world of Barbie struggled to fill its space in the cavernous Broward County Convention Center. “It felt like being just trapped in a big, dark warehouse,” one attendee said.

item.title: Employee Lawsuit Against Fox News Is Dismissed
item.description: A reporter who claimed the network had fired him for challenging its coverage failed to prove retaliation and discrimination, a federal judge said.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021. Jason Donner, a 12-year employee, accused Fox News of firing him for raising concerns about its coverage of the Capitol riot and the 2020 presidential election.

item.title: The Army Begins an Inquiry After Kid Rock Gets Apache Helicopter Flyby
item.description: The pro-Trump musician saluted the pair of Apache attack choppers, which appeared to be the same ones that flew low over a “No Kings” rally in Nashville.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: George Walker IV/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A military helicopter flew over a “No Kings” protest on Saturday in Nashville.

item.title: A Crash Course on Primary Elections in Maine, Michigan and Beyond
item.description: Here’s a crash course on what’s coming up next.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Robert F. Bukaty/Associated Press; Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Democratic primary race for Senate in Maine is between Graham Platner and Gov. Janet Mills

item.title: A New U.S. Missile Hit a Sports Hall in Iran. Here’s What We Know About It.
item.description: Barely out of prototype testing, the Precision Strike Missile is shrouded in secrecy — including which Persian Gulf countries the Army is launching them from.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Mexico Pressures U.S. Over Deaths of Its Citizens in ICE Custody
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum, president of Mexico, said her country would take legal steps to demand better conditions at immigration detention facilities, where she said 14 Mexican citizens had died since President Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Jazmine Ulloa and Allison McCann

media:content.media:credit: Quetzalli Nicte-Ha/Reuters
media:content.media:description: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico at a news conference on Monday.

item.title: Michigan Synagogue Attack Was ‘Inspired by Hezbollah,’ Officials Say
item.description: The man who rammed his vehicle into the synagogue was purposely targeting the Jewish community, officials said on Monday, detailing his days of planning.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, Mich.

item.title: Weekly Student News Quiz: Social Media Trial, Global Protests, Baseball
item.description: Have you been paying attention to current events recently? See how many of these 10 questions you can get right.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Engle

media:content.media:credit: Brad Penner/Imagn Images, via Imagn Images Via Reuters Connect

item.title: Ben Stevenson Dies at 89; Choreographer Made Houston Ballet Thrive
item.description: He created dances performed around the world, and under his leadership the Houston company grew into one of America’s largest and most prominent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Seibert

media:content.media:credit: Jack Mitchell/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Ben Stevenson near the end of his dancing career in Britain. Beginning in the early ’70s, he spent decades leading dance companies in the United States.

item.title: Celine Dion vuelve a los escenarios
item.description: La cantante canadiense, quien ha aparecido pocas veces en el escenario desde que anunció su diagnóstico de síndrome de la persona rígida en 2022, ofrecerá 10 espectáculos a partir de septiembre.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Ord/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Celine Dion celebró su cumpleaños el lunes con el anuncio de que volverá a los escenarios en París.

item.title: Mark Sanford, Years After Scandal, Aims for a Comeback to Congress
item.description: The former South Carolina governor and congressman filed papers to run for his old seat, six years after running for president and nearly two decades after a high-profile affair.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Boucher/The State — Tribune News Service, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mark Sanford in 2023. His star has risen and fallen in Republican politics over the years.

item.title: ¿Por qué EE. UU. permitió que llegue petróleo de Rusia a Cuba en medio del bloqueo?
item.description: La llegada de un buque a Cuba le dará el combustible que tanto necesita. También representa un cambio en los esfuerzos estadounidenses por bloquear los envíos de petróleo y presionar al gobierno cubano.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and David C. Adams

media:content.media:credit: Planet Labs
media:content.media:description: Una imagen de satélite tomada la semana pasada del puerto y la terminal petrolera a la entrada de la bahía de Matanzas, en el noroeste de Cuba.

item.title: Trump Zigzags on Iran
item.description: Also, T.S.A. workers start getting back pay. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Trump Reveals New Look for White House Ballroom After Times Article
item.description: Architects and preservation organizations have warned that little scrutiny has been given to the project’s design plans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Badger

media:content.media:credit: Elizabeth Frantz/Reuters
media:content.media:description: President Trump showing a new rendering of the proposed White House ballroom’s south portico, without the grand staircase that appeared in previous plans.

item.title: What to Know About California’s Executive Order on A.I.
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, issued an order requiring safety and privacy guardrails for artificial intelligence companies contracting with the state.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom. California has been a leader in tech lawmaking, and was the first state to pass a law mandating safety and transparency from the biggest A.I. companies.

item.title: Woman Spent Five Months in Jail After A.I. Linked Her to Bank Fraud Case
item.description: The police chief in Fargo, N.D., acknowledged “missteps” but stopped short of apologizing to Angela Lipps, a Tennessee resident who said she had never been to North Dakota before she was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: Angela Lipps
media:content.media:description: Angela Lipps outside her home in Elizabethton, Tenn., before she was arrested in July 2025. She spent five months in jail in Tennessee and North Dakota before she was released on Dec. 24.

item.title: Cuba sufre la presión de Trump. Pero en Guantánamo, el ejército de EE. UU. vive tranquilo
item.description: La base de la Marina en la bahía de Guantánamo brilla por la noche mientras los vecinos al otro lado de un campo de minas cubano batallan en la oscuridad, víctimas de un embargo de combustible.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Carol Rosenberg/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un boliche o bolera en la base militar de Guantánamo un sábado. La vida en la base no se ha visto afectada en su mayor parte por la crisis energética en Cuba.

item.title: America Is Abandoning Morality
item.description: The bombing campaign Mr. Trump is threatening in Iran could affect millions of Iranian civilians’ access to water, electricity and food.
item.dc:creator: W.J. Hennigan
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Palm Sunday Attack in Nigeria Leaves at Least 12 Dead
item.description: No one has claimed responsibility for the killings in the mostly Christian city of Jos, and the police have yet to arrest the gunmen.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: A Nigerian military vehicle at the scene of an attack the morning after gunmen killed several people in Jos, Nigeria, on Monday.

item.title: Mary Beth Hurt, Actress Acclaimed in ‘Interiors’ and ‘Garp,’ Dies at 79
item.description: She elevated supporting film roles with insight and improvisational skill, a talent she took to Broadway as well, earning Tony nominations.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Vermont Hits Back at Trump’s Effort to Block ‘Climate Superfund’ Law
item.description: The law would make fuel companies help pay for damages caused by climate change. The administration argues it’s unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick

media:content.media:credit: Caleb Kenna for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The federal courthouse in Rutland, Vt., on Monday, where the Justice Department was suing to block the state’s superfund law.

item.title: Removing Unfriendly Leaders
item.description: The Trump administration appears to have embraced a new foreign policy tactic: getting rid of leaders it doesn’t like. Here’s how that can go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: States Plow Ahead With A.I. Regulation, Defying Trump
item.description: States ranging from California to Utah are taking steps to place guardrails on the technology even after the president ordered them to stop.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang

media:content.media:credit: Max Whittaker for The New York Times

item.title: Don’t Know Where to Go This Summer? Let Us Help!
item.description: We’re responding to readers’ travel quandaries with our suggestions.
item.dc:creator: Deborah Dunn

media:content.media:credit: Roxanne McCann/Getty Images

item.title: Kuwaiti Tanker Full of Oil Struck Off Dubai Day After Trump’s Threats
item.description: The vessel caught fire and sustained damage, its owner said on Tuesday, adding that there was the potential for an oil spill in surrounding waters. On Monday, President Trump warned Iran that without a deal he would order attacks on infrastructure targets.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
